title: Effects of Federal Policy to Insure Young Adults: Evidence from the 2010 Affordable Care Act's Dependent-Coverage Mandate
notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. Akosa Antwi, Moriya & Simon (2013, AEJ:EP). Stata: xi: areg anyhi mar_sep10 after_oct10 fedelig elig_mar10 elig_oct10 age17-age25 age27-age29 trend female ue hispanic white asian other student mar fpl_ratio fpl_ratio_2 ue_treat y2009 y2010 month2-month12 st_trend_1-st_trend_55 [weight=p_weight], absorb(fipstate) cluster(fipstate). Treatment = fedelig (age 19-25) x after_oct10 (post Oct 2010). SIPP data, all ages 16-29. Template simplification: uses fipstate + yearmonth + age FEs (more saturated than Stata which uses fipstate + year + month + trend + state-specific trends). State-specific linear trends NOT included — may cause discrepancy. CS-DID: gvar_CS=34 (Oct 2010 as yearmonth) for treated (fedelig=1), 0 for never-treated. | AUDIT 2026-04-19: Paper-auditor EXACT (0.18%). Lesson 7 direct-level collapse #6 (12 RCS individual ctrls single-cohort, Spec A → 0/NA). TWFE robust across specs (0.032 with vs 0.034 no ctrls). HonestDiD D-FRAGILE Mbar=0 (structural RCS individual-level SEs). LOW driven by design fragility + CS-DID uninformative SE.
